{"key_event":"Retreat from Reconstruction after 1877","reasoning":"Federal institutions remained strong by antebellum standards, with the DOJ, rail-linked markets, western military campaigns, and strike suppression showing substantial capacity, but the withdrawal from Reconstruction and Posse Comitatus marked a real retreat from enforcing national authority inside the South. Civil society stayed very strong overall through parties, press, corporations, churches, and emerging labor organization, yet Black and Native political capacity was sharply weakened by paramilitary violence, court limits, and federal abandonment.","society_change":-0.4,"society_power":8.1,"state_change":-0.4,"state_power":7.4}
